relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Self-love, money-love, pride, and ingratitude distort human life.
Affection, reconciliation, self-control, and love of good disappear in the distorted pattern.
Pleasure replaces love of the Divine.
A form of reverence without power must be turned from.
Predatory teachers enter households and exploit vulnerability.
As Jannes and Jambres opposed Moses, corrupted minds oppose truth.
Timothy has followed teaching, conduct, purpose, trust, patience, love, and endurance.
Paul’s sufferings were real, and the Lord rescued him.
Reverent life in Messiah may meet opposition.
Timothy remains in what he learned.
Sacred writings have shaped him from infancy toward saving wisdom.
Scripture is Divine-breathed and useful for teaching, correction, and training in justice.
The human of Divine is equipped for every beautiful work.

contemplative_note: 2 Timothy 3 protects discernment: avoid powerless religiosity, name predatory teaching, remain in sacred wisdom, and let Divine-breathed Scripture equip beautiful work.
